en.extract:
The star-spotted nightjar (Caprimulgus stellatus) is a species of nightjar in the family Caprimulgidae.
It is found in central Ethiopia, northern and northwestern Kenya and southern South Sudan.
Star-spotted nightjar tend to inhabit low-altitude and dry habitats such as the lava-strewn fields, stony or sandy semi-deserts, drawf grassland as well as open bushland.
Star-spotted nightjar share physical similarity with Plain nightjar (Caprimulgus inornatus) as they are both small to medium sized with proportionately large heads. Except the star-spotted nightjar have distinct white patch on its throat separated by dark line and corner of its tails.

The '''star-spotted nightjar''' ('''''Caprimulgus stellatus''''') is a species of [[nightjar]] in the family [[Caprimulgidae]].
It is found in central [[Ethiopia]], northern and northwestern [[Kenya]] and southern [[South Sudan]].

Star-spotted nightjar tend to inhabit low-altitude and dry habitats such as the lava-strewn fields, stony or sandy semi-deserts, drawf grassland as well as open bushland.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Mills |first1=Michael S L |last2=Spottiswoode |first2=Claire N |date=September 2000 |title=Star-spotted Nightjar |url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/part/309618 |journal=Bulletin of the African Bird Club |volume=7 |issue=2 |pages=141–143 |doi=10.5962/p.309618 |issn=1352-481X|doi-access=free }}</ref>

Star-spotted nightjar share physical similarity with [[Plain nightjar|Plain nightjar (''Caprimulgus inornatus'')]] as they are both small to medium sized with proportionately large heads.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last1=Mills |first1=Michael Stuart Lyne |last2=Mills |first2=Michael Stuart Lyne |last3=Spottiswoode |first3=Claire N. |date=September 2000 |title=Star-spotted Nightjar |url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/part/309618 |journal=Bulletin of the African Bird Club |volume=7 |issue=2 |pages=141––143 |doi=10.5962/p.309618|doi-access=free }}</ref> Except the star-spotted nightjar have distinct white patch on its throat separated by dark line and corner of its tails.<ref name=":1" />
